pi_items.source: setup_task.sh explicitly writes this target name into seti_alert_protocol.txt
setup_task.sh writes RA 20h 06m 15.45s into seti_alert_protocol.txt and verifier.py checks against 20.1043
setup_task.sh writes Dec +44d 27m 24.6s into seti_alert_protocol.txt and verifier.py checks against 44.4568
setup_task.sh and verifier.py explicitly require NAXIS1=512 and NAXIS2=512 for the generated subframes
setup_task.sh requires exactly 1.0 second exposure time and verifier.py enforces it
setup_task.sh and verifier.py require at least 20 valid FITS frames
setup_task.sh explicitly creates this directory and export_result.sh scans it for FITS files
setup_task.sh creates the protocol file exactly at this path
Matches the implied role in the prompt and verifier.py (Astronomer / Optical SETI Researcher)
Matches the implied context in the prompt and verifier.py (Optical SETI Researcher, rapid high-cadence imaging)

privileged_info_summary: I verified that the task operates within a simulated environment using synthetic alerts and seeded files. The setup scripts configure a fake alert protocol for KIC 8462852 (Boyajian's Star) at RA 20.1043 hours and Dec 44.4568 degrees. The verifier accurately enforces the requirements from the setup scripts: the agent must produce at least 20 new FITS files with an exposure time of 1.0s and a subframed ROI of exactly 512x512 pixels in the target directory /home/ga/Images/oseti/kic8462852, within a 30 arcmin tolerance.
